name: SEABOARD CORP /DE/
summary: Seaboard Corporation is a diversified conglomerate operating in agricultural, energy, and ocean transport businesses. It engages in hog production, pork processing, and biodiesel/renewable diesel production in the U.S.; commodity trading and grain processing in Africa and South America; cargo shipping services in the Caribbean and Central and South America; electric power generation in the Dominican Republic; and holds a 52.5% equity investment in Butterball, LLC, a turkey products producer.

products.name: Pork Products
products.description: Pork sold to further processors, foodservice operators, distributors, and grocery stores in U.S. and internationally.

products.name: Renewable Natural Gas
products.description: Swine-derived renewable natural gas produced from hog operations and anaerobic digester lagoons at farms in Texas, Oklahoma, and Kansas.

products.name: Biodiesel and Renewable Diesel
products.description: Biodiesel produced in Oklahoma and Missouri; renewable diesel produced in Kansas under Seaboard Energy, LLC.

products.name: Cargo Shipping Services
products.description: Dry, refrigerated, and other cargo shipping between the U.S. and 27 countries in the Caribbean and Central and South America.

products.name: Electric Power Generation
products.description: Electricity generated for the Dominican Republic power grid via two barges, EDM II and EDM III.

products.name: Turkey Products
products.description: Turkey products supplied to retail stores, foodservice outlets, and industrial entities via equity investment in Butterball, LLC.

products.name: Agricultural Commodity Trading and Milling
products.description: Trading of grains and oilseeds, milling of flour, animal feed, and animal-based proteins across 26 countries primarily in Africa and South America.

products.name: Sugar and Alcohol
products.description: Sugar and alcohol produced at a vertically integrated facility in Argentina with nearly 70,000 acres of cultivated sugarcane land.

products.name: Environmental Credits (RINs, LCFS)
products.description: Renewable identification numbers and Low Carbon Fuel Standard credits generated from biofuel production and sold to third parties.

partnerships.description: 50% joint venture partner in Seaboard Triumph Foods, LLC (STF), operating a pork processing plant in Iowa.
50% co-owner in Dailys Premium Meats, LLC, producing raw and pre-cooked bacon in Utah, Montana, and Missouri.
50% co-owner in Seaboard de Mexico USA LLC, operating a ham-boning and processing plant in Mexico.
Seaboard holds a non-controlling 52.5% equity method investment in Butterball, a national turkey products supplier.
